findings.title: Read-only tool requests dangerous parameters
findings.detail: Tool "get_contact" appears informational but accepts parameters (code) that enable code/file/secret access.

name: report_contact_outcome
description: One-tap outcome report on an accepted contact: did it lead to a hire? Optional but appreciated — it's how the marketplace measures that consented outreach beats cold outreach. Values: hired | not_hired | in_progress.

name: get_new_candidates
description: Get resumes new or updated since a timestamp (your standing alert). Same filters as search_candidates. Call daily with yesterday's timestamp; no duplicates.

name: save_candidate
description: Save a candidate resume to your watchlist with optional notes.

name: get_corpus_stats
description: Get aggregate stats about the talent corpus (counts, top skills/locations).

name: check_subscription
description: Check your subscription tier, status, rate limit, and usage this hour.

name: get_docs
description: Get the full QueryQuarry reference (how it works, all tools and filters, search tips, rate limits, privacy). Call this when you need detail beyond these tool descriptions, when guiding a new user, or before composing a complex search.

findings.title: Read-only tool requests dangerous parameters
findings.detail: Tool "get_contact" appears informational but accepts parameters (code) that enable code/file/secret access.

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: request_contact, get_contact · Private data: search_candidates, get_candidate, request_contact, get_contact, report_contact_outcome · Public sink: request_contact, get_contact.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: request_contact, get_contact · Private data: search_candidates, get_candidate, request_contact, get_contact, report_contact_outcome · Public sink: request_contact, get_contact

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
